design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: Flagship upgrade of ordinal principal-strata partial identification: derive an explicit K=3 compatibility-facet certificate and phase threshold for strict nonrectangularity beyond pairwise adjacent-stratum bounds. Pre-anchor check: closest banked result is pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1, accepted only at field tier with an adjacent-chain posterior LP; closest published anchors are Frangakis-Rubin principal stratification and ordinal principal-score / principal-ignorability work. Why non-trivial? The target is not a new-domain LP dual or another Manski interval: require a named facet normal, a minimal K=3 witness law, and a proof that the shared middle stratum creates strict containment of the common-law feasible polytope inside all pairwise relaxations, with an endpoint-attaining certificate. Why promising? The nonroutine object is the hand-derived compatibility facet and strict-containment witness. If the delta reduces to LP duality, support coverage, or definition-unfold frontier algebra, reject or downgrade.

proposed_from.topic: Flagship upgrade of ordinal principal-strata partial identification: derive an explicit K=3 compatibility-facet certificate and phase threshold for strict nonrectangularity beyond pairwise adjacent-stratum bounds. Pre-anchor check: closest banked result is pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1, accepted only at field tier with an adjacent-chain posterior LP; closest published anchors are Frangakis-Rubin principal stratification and ordinal principal-score / principal-ignorability work. Why non-trivial? The target is not a new-domain LP dual or another Manski interval: require a named facet normal, a minimal K=3 witness law, and a proof that the shared middle stratum creates strict containment of the common-law feasible polytope inside all pairwise relaxations, with an endpoint-attaining certificate. Why promising? The nonroutine object is the hand-derived compatibility facet and strict-containment witness. If the delta reduces to LP duality, support coverage, or definition-unfold frontier algebra, reject or downgrade.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue prior is the parent pid_ordinal_principal_strata/v1, whose headline was a sharp finite-support adjacent-chain posterior LP with a nonrectangular ordinal compatibility gap. This revision keeps that parent as Theorem 1 and makes the upgrade delta explicit: Conjecture 1 names C_mid and n_mid and asks for an irredundant K=3 facet certificate; Conjecture 2 asks for the primitive symmetric witness slack and active-basis phase, not merely another LP interval.","published_axis":"FrangakisRubin2002 and DingLu2017 anchor the common principal-strata law and principal-score identification under PI. LuZhangDing2020 is the closest endpoint-construction comparator for ordinal bounds, but it couples ordinal potential-outcome marginals rather than posterior principal-strata chains. TongKahanHarhayLi2025 gives an odds-ratio sensitivity frontier for principal stratification beyond monotonicity, but not the fixed-pi K=3 shared-middle compatibility facet or the Delta_mid_sym phase certificate."}
seed_list: Parent has qualitative nonrectangularity; this upgrade delivers a named K=3 middle-facet certificate C_mid with normal e_b^1 and endpoint-attaining rational law.
Parent has strict-gap arithmetic; this upgrade packages it as a minimal K=3 endpoint witness separating T_mid from T_pair.
Parent has local continuity strictness; this upgrade conjectures a phase scalar Phi_mid deciding the relative-open strict-containment regime.
Parent assumes fixed pi; this upgrade keeps fixed pi to isolate compatibility geometry from principal-score uncertainty and auxiliary-variable identification.

seed_details.one_line: Parent has qualitative nonrectangularity; this upgrade delivers a named K=3 middle-facet certificate C_mid with normal e_b^1 and endpoint-attaining rational law.

anchor_paper.author: Frangakis and Rubin; Ding and Lu; parent proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Common latent principal-strata law and principal-score mixtures must underlie adjacent projections.
anchor_paper.location: FrangakisRubin2002 p.21; DingLu2017 Section 2; parent Conjecture 1
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: shared middle stratum creates compatibility facets that are absent from any product

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Default-to-LP risk; strengthened by naming a facet normal and endpoint certificate rather than another LP dual.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the parent LP because every LP endpoint has a supporting hyperplane.
seed_details.rebuttal: The parent did not name the hyperplane, prove irredundancy, or give the middle-coordinate facet certificate as the theorem-level object.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not pid_ordinal_principal_strata_v1 because the parent proves sharpness and strictness but does not isolate C_mid as a named mechanism facet.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: routine LP geometry is used only after a new focal object, C_mid, and a worked endpoint witness are specified.

adversarial_referee.instance: Facet normal e_b^1 may be too simple to clear the focal-object floor.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the normal is paired with a nontrivial derivation certificate and endpoint witness.

adversarial_referee.instance: Validity alone is not flagship without irredundancy.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 1 explicitly requires irredundancy and active-face attainment.

adversarial_referee.instance: Symmetric K=3 witness might look hand-tuned.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 2 promotes the witness to a relative-open phase neighborhood.

seed_details.burned_reason: Angle 0 reached D-0.5 flagship acceptance but D0.5 rejected it as below the flagship floor; future retries need a derived generic-class facet/phase certificate, not this symmetric-cell upgrade.

seed_details.one_line: Parent has strict-gap arithmetic; this upgrade packages it as a minimal K=3 endpoint witness separating T_mid from T_pair.

anchor_paper.author: Lu, Zhang, and Ding; parent reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Ordinal sharp bounds value explicit endpoint-attaining constructions; parent reviewer requested explicit K=3 finite-support witness.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Add an explicit K=3 finite-support witness showing strict containment

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample risk; strengthened by a non-corner rational witness plus internal consistency checks.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just the parent witness copied into a proposal.
seed_details.rebuttal: The witness is promoted from arithmetic evidence to an exhibit computing C_mid, T_mid, T_pair, and Phi_mid from the same primitives.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not the parent witness because the upgrade packages the active facet and phase value, not only strict inequality.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the witness computes distinct coherent and pairwise endpoints and rules out embedding by the facet.

adversarial_referee.instance: Y=0 in one arm could make the causal contrast degenerate.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The facet is a treated-cell compatibility object; the opposite arm is only used to expose endpoint separation.

adversarial_referee.instance: LuZhangDing2020 concerns ordinal outcomes, not principal strata.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposal cites it for endpoint-certificate standards, not as a direct comparator.

adversarial_referee.instance: Parent derivation may already contain the witness arithmetic.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Acknowledged; the delta is the named certificate and phase threshold, not raw arithmetic.

seed_details.one_line: Parent has local continuity strictness; this upgrade conjectures a phase scalar Phi_mid deciding the relative-open strict-containment regime.

anchor_paper.author: Lu, Zhang, and Ding; Tong et al.; parent derivation
anchor_paper.claim: Closed-form ordinal thresholds and odds-ratio sensitivity motivate a phase scalar rather than a numerical LP comparison.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: binary upper value exceeds the ordinal upper value by (Gamma+2)/(3(Gamma+1)(2Gamma+1))

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Critical-threshold risk; strengthened by computing Phi_mid from two endpoint formulas and requiring active-basis stability.
seed_details.referee_objection: Phi_mid is defined as the endpoint difference, so the iff is tautological.
seed_details.rebuttal: The conjecture requires independently computed coherent and pairwise endpoints from cell primitives, plus active-basis stability, not a definition-only iff.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not parent continuity because it names the phase scalar and the active-basis condition where the facet first binds.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if the phase proof derives endpoints and basis stability; lands as field-tier if left as Phi equals difference by definition.

adversarial_referee.instance: The converse from Phi_mid positive to strict containment may be underproved.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture label is honest; proof route names incompatibility and active-basis steps.

adversarial_referee.instance: Critical scalar may be only local symmetric-cell algebra.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Scope flags state the arbitrary unequal-cell extension may be piecewise.

adversarial_referee.instance: Positive Phi may be sufficient but not necessary outside symmetric cells.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The current flagship claim is local to the nondegenerate phase neighborhood, not global over all laws.

seed_details.one_line: Parent assumes fixed pi; this upgrade keeps fixed pi to isolate compatibility geometry from principal-score uncertainty and auxiliary-variable identification.

anchor_paper.author: Feller, Mealli, and Miratrix; Jiang and Ding; parent proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Principal ignorability and auxiliary variables are stronger routes, while the target here is fixed-pi compatibility geometry.
anchor_paper.location: Stage -1.1 open_problem[3]; parent known principal-score assumption
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: pi(x) is known or supplied by external design information

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assumption-weakening risk; this is a scope-control move rather than the flagship kernel.
seed_details.referee_objection: Keeping fixed pi is not an upgrade mechanism.
seed_details.rebuttal: Correct; it is retained to prevent the mechanism theorem from being confounded with pi-polytope projection.

proposed_from.literature_map: Stage -1.1 supplied the literature map. Verified reused anchors include FrangakisRubin2002 for principal strata as joint potential post-treatment values, DingLu2017 for principal-score identification under principal ignorability, and LuZhangDing2020 for explicit sharp ordinal endpoint constructions. Reused parent anchors also include VanderWeele2011 on dichotomization limits, WangZhangMealliBornkamp2023 as a binary PI-sensitivity workflow consumer, and WuAntonelli2024 on PI-violation partial identification. New frontier anchor: TongKahanHarhayLi2025, Statistica Sinica forthcoming, uses conditional odds-ratio sensitivity for semiparametric principal stratification beyond monotonicity, but does not give a K=3 fixed-pi finite-cell compatibility facet. Source URLs consulted include https://academic.oup.com/jrsssb/article/79/3/757/7040654, https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4137767/, https://ideas.repec.org/a/bla/biomet/v76y2020i2p664-669.html, and https://www3.stat.sinica.edu.tw/statistica/fp/SS-2025-0066.html.

banked_reason: D0.5 rejected the flagship upgrade on novelty: the K=3 middle-facet algebra was coherent, but the contribution remained a symmetric finite-cell LP endpoint calculation and imported the open-neighborhood phase through an assumption rather than deriving a generic-class theorem.

seeds_burned.one_liner: Parent has qualitative nonrectangularity; this upgrade delivers a named K=3 middle-facet certificate C_mid with normal e_b^1 and endpoint-attaining rational law.
seeds_burned.reason: Angle 0 reached D-0.5 flagship acceptance but D0.5 rejected it as below the flagship floor; future retries need a derived generic-class facet/phase certificate, not this symmetric-cell upgrade.
